---
title: "UCSB Undergraduate Research Slam"
description: 2024 Research Champion
categories: Presentations
image: media/Slam.PNG
---
![Can you say *Stereolepis gigas* three times fast?](media/Slam.PNG)

Out of 15 finalists, I placed first in UC Santa Barbara's 2024 [Undergraduate Research Slam](https://urca.ucsb.edu/urca-week/slam). The Undergraduate Research Slam is a competition where participants have just three minutes and three slides to give a dynamic presentation of their research project. I presented my talk "Uncovering the Mysteries of the Giant Sea Bass: Integrating Technology and Community Science for Conservation". Watch my presentation [here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-3eC0VqSbA0)!

I was awarded a $500 scholarship and presented my talk to UCSB's Chancellor and Board of Trustees at their May meeting. I also had the opportunity to judge the final round of UC Santa Barbara's 2025 [Graduate Research Slam](https://gradslam.ucsb.edu/past-showcases/2025/final-round/macey-hartmann).
